channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Elevated error rates for Sonnet 5
item.description: Jul 22, 16:12 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 22, 15:36 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 22, 15:24 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Elevated errors for Workspace Agent
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Agent (Operational)

item.title: Open models recap: more on Kimi K3, Qwen 3.8, Xi's WAIC speech, distillation, the open-closed gap, and what's next
item.description: A podcast with Florian Brand.

item.title: When Identity Verification Fails: Lessons from a Real-World SIM Swap and Near Account Takeover
item.description: Identity confidence changes throughout every interaction and should be reassessed continuously as new risk signals emerge. The post When Identity Verification Fails: Lessons from a Real-World SIM Swap and Near Account Takeover appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Accelerating the frontiers of scientific discovery: Google’s $40M commitment to the Genesis Mission
item.description: Google commits $40M in AI tokens and credits for the Genesis Mission

item.title: Elevated errors on Opus 4.8 and Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jul 22, 13:37 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Hackers Exploit Windmill Flaw to Read Arbitrary Server Files Without Authentication
item.description: A high-severity security flaw impacting open-source developer platform Windmill has come under active exploitation in the wild, per VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-29059 (CVSS score: 7.5), a case of unauthenticated path traversal impacting Windmill's "get_log_file" endpoint...

item.title: The Download: NASA’s new space telescope and OpenAI’s autonomous hacker
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Shape-shifting mirrors on NASA’s new space telescope could unveil Jupiters like our own When NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope launches, as...

item.title: Post-quantum cryptography (PQC) migration workshop report
item.description: No organisation can navigate the migration alone; key takeaways from our first PQC migration workshop.

item.title: Endpoint Security Firm Glow Launches With $180M in Funding at $1.2B Valuation
item.description: Using AI, the startup provides adaptive prevention through environment mapping, risk analysis, and automated policy enforcement. The post Endpoint Security Firm Glow Launches With $180M in Funding at $1.2B Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Increase in errors from R2 in ENAM
item.description: Jul 22, 09:55 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 22, 09:20 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 22, 08:22 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is aware of, and investigating an issue with an increased number of...

item.title: Elevated Errors Affecting File Uploads and Image Generation
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components File uploads (Degraded performance) Image Generation (Degraded performance)

item.title: Oracle Patches Over 1,400 Vulnerabilities With Quarterly Security Updates
item.description: Many of the vulnerabilities fixed with the July 2026 Critical Patch Update were likely discovered by AI. The post Oracle Patches Over 1,400 Vulnerabilities With Quarterly Security Updates appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.1
item.description: Jul 22, 09:00 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 22, 08:45 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 22, 08:34 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: Shape-shifting mirrors on NASA’s new space telescope could unveil Jupiters like our own
item.description: When NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope launches, as early as the end of next month, it will attempt one of astronomy’s most precise disappearing acts to date. The telescope will carry the first space-bound “active” coronagraph, an instrument that effectively erases most of the light from a...

item.title: Ransomware Group Threatening to Leak Data Stolen From Coca-Cola’s Fairlife
item.description: The Anubis ransomware group claims to have stolen 1 TB of confidential data from the Coca-Cola subsidiary. The post Ransomware Group Threatening to Leak Data Stolen From Coca-Cola’s Fairlife appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Xiaohongshu Engine Team Publishes HELMSMAN at OSDI 2026: Reinventing Billion-Scale Vector Search Infrastructure on All-Flash Storage
item.description: Xiaohongshu engine team publishes HELMSMAN at OSDI 2026, replacing 35K CPU cores and 350TB DRAM with 40 all-flash servers for billion-scale vector search at 90% cost reduction.

item.title: Unitree and AgiBot Open Retail Storefronts: Humanoid Robots Move From Laboratory Leaderboards to Shopping Mall Showrooms as Competition Shifts to Physical Retail
item.description: Unitree Robotics and AgiBot open flagship stores in prime shopping districts, testing the complete commerce loop from customer experience to purchase to after-sales service for humanoid robots.

item.title: Orienspace Gravity-1 Succeeds in First Deep-Sea Launch: Three More Commercial Batch Launches Planned in 2026 Including Satellite Internet Constellation
item.description: Orienspace completes first deep-sea launch of Gravity-1 carrying 9 satellites, enters scaled commercial operations, with Gravity-2 reusable liquid rocket targeting Q4 2026 first flight.

item.title: The Physical AI Closed Loop Is Finally Live: Rimnot and Yuantu Announce 10K-Unit Industrial Robot Deployment Plan for Server Manufacturing
item.description: Rimnot, a 4-month-old physical AI startup founded by Tsinghua PhD alumni, partners with Yuantu for 10K-unit robot deployment in server manufacturing, achieving 30-minute environment adaptation at WAIC.

item.title: Tencent Quietly Launches Miora: Its First Full-Stack Design Agent Platform Now Open to All After Invite-Only Beta
item.description: Tencent WorkBuddy team launches Miora, a full-stack design agent platform covering brand design, UI, e-commerce, and interactive creation with multi-model support and skill library.

item.title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Broke Loose and Hacked Hugging Face
item.description: OpenAI says its AI models went rogue, as CISOS call the incident a watershed moment, warning that autonomous AI threat models have officially crossed into production reality. The post OpenAI Says Its AI Models Broke Loose and Hacked Hugging Face appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Istanbul
item.description: Jul 22, 07:44 UTC Resolved - Cloudflare is aware of Network Performance issues in Istanbul, Türkiye, between 08:44 and 08:50 UTC.Service has recovered, and operations are back to normal.

item.title: Police Dismantle Kratos Phishing Kit Built to Steal Microsoft 365 Sessions and Bypass MFA
item.description: German and US law enforcement have taken down the core infrastructure of Kratos, described by German investigators as one of the world's most widely used criminal phishing kits, and Indonesian authorities arrested the man they say developed and ran it. In a joint announcement on Monday, the...

item.title: Cloudflare Malicious Upload Detection Issues
item.description: Jul 22, 06:27 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare Malicious Upload Detection IssuesCloudflare is investigating issues with elevated errors in Malicious Upload Detection service.We are working to understand the full impact and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Trojanized Newtonsoft.Json Fork Hides Game-Rigging Code in a Working Library
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a NuGet typosquat that's unlike the typical information-stealing malware distributed via package registries: usual info-stealers: it's designed to rig live game results on Digitain. The package, named "Newtonsoftt.Json.Net," masquerades as the...

item.title: Microsoft Azure DevOps MCP Flaw Lets Hidden PR Comments Hijack AI Review Agents
item.description: A single invisible comment in an Azure DevOps pull request can turn a reviewer's own AI coding agent against them, driving it into projects the attacker has no rights to reach and quietly leaking what it finds. The flaw is in Microsoft's official Azure DevOps MCP server, and it works because one of...

item.title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Escaped Sandbox, Targeted Hugging Face to Cheat Benchmark
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday said a combination of its artificial intelligence (AI) models, including GPT-5.6 Sol and an "even more capable pre-release model," was behind the security incident that targeted Hugging Face's production infrastructure last week. The AI company said the models were operating with...

item.title: SysAdmin: Measuring Instrumental Power-Seeking in Frontier AI
item.description: arXiv:2607.18239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Power-seeking defined as behaviors where AI systems acquire resources, evade oversight, or resist termination beyond task requirements is identified as a key driver of Loss of Control (LoC) risk. In this work, we introduce SysAdmin, a benchmark that...

item.title: Calibrated Selective Fact-Checking via Evidence Chain Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2607.18240v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can achieve strong fact-checking accuracy, yet forced binary decisions conceal a critical reliability problem: systems may issue confident verdicts even when supporting evidence is weak, sparse, or internally inconsistent....

item.title: BatchDAG: LLM-Planned Execution Graphs for Scalable Ad-Hoc Analysis Over Enterprise Data
item.description: arXiv:2607.18241v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) excel at analyzing individual documents but break down on exhaustive, cross-entity analytical questions over enterprise-scale datasets due to context overflow, loss of per-entity attribution, and linear latency from...

item.title: AI Tool Discovery at Scale: All You Need is DNS
item.description: arXiv:2607.18242v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The coming era of autonomous AI agents demands a discovery mechanism capable of navigating millions of tools, yet existing solutions buckle under O(N) complexity and centralized governance. Instead of building another fragile overlay, we propose...

item.title: From Agent Failure Paths to Quantified Residual Risk: A Compositional Framework for Resilient Agentic AI
item.description: arXiv:2607.18243v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic AI is crossing trust boundaries faster than current risk models can represent. Existing approaches provide one of two partial views. They either describe failure mechanisms without producing a transferable residual-risk estimate, or they...

item.title: FALCON-Discover: Discovering Concentrated False-Confidence Regions for Calibration
item.description: arXiv:2607.18278v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Calibration is usually evaluated in aggregate, but the most dangerous failures are often local: predictions that remain highly confident despite being wrong. We study this failure mode as false-confidence concentration, the extent to which confident...

item.title: Beyond Output-Space Calibration: Spectral Evidence Bundling for Selective Reliability Estimation in Time-Series Classification
item.description: arXiv:2607.18279v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Post-hoc calibration for time-series classification usually remaps output scores, but deployment decisions such as trust, abstention, and review depend on whether a confident prediction is supported by the current temporal signal. We address three...

item.title: Beyond Single-Dimensional Compression: The Compound Sparsity Frontier of Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.18280v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are often compressed through static parameter pruning or dynamic token-level computation, yet aggressive sparsification can trigger rapid performance degradation beyond an essential sparsity boundary. This work asks...

item.title: ALAS: Additive Learnable Alpha-Stable Kernels for Flexible Bayesian Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2607.18282v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Bayesian Optimization is widely used for expensive black-box optimization, yet its success often depends on choosing a kernel that matches the objective's unknown structure. In this work, we propose ALAS, a flexible Gaussian Process kernel family...

item.title: FedCC: A Low-Resource Federated Adaptation of Foundation Models for Robust Corpus Callosum localization in Fetal Ultrasound Images
item.description: arXiv:2607.18283v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate localization of the corpus callosum (CC) in fetal ultrasound (US) images is crucial for the early identification of neurodevelopmental abnormalities. However, this task remains highly challenging due to the intrinsic limitations of US...

item.title: Decoding EEG Signals to Explore Next-Word Predictability in the Human Brain
item.description: arXiv:2607.18321v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Humans invented reading and have passed down this complex skill across generations through language. This study provides empirical evidence of the neural mechanisms underlying bottom-up (related to high-order linguistic structure) and top-down...

item.title: A Classifier That Teaches Itself: Self-Improving, Frozen-gate Training (SIFT) for Dynamic Document Classification
item.description: arXiv:2607.18358v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Document classification is a solved problem in the laboratory and an unsolved one in the enterprise. The blocker is rarely model architecture; it is the labeling project that must precede a model and the institutional fear of letting a model retrain...

item.title: Convolution for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.18413v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) largely rely on Transformers, where self-attention provides global token interaction but does not explicitly encode the locality of natural language. We study whether lightweight depthwise convolutions can supply this...

item.title: Building a European Multilingual Evaluation Dataset: The MMLU Localisation Project within the EMT Network
item.description: arXiv:2607.18432v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper reports on a collaboration between the Directorate-General for Translation (DGT) and the European Master's in Translation (EMT) to localise the MMLU dataset into 11 European languages. Beyond creating a more inclusive benchmark for LLM...

item.title: Relay-Bench: Evaluating LLMs on Multi-Domain Reasoning Chains
item.description: arXiv:2607.18438v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Introducing Relay-Bench, an unsaturated, holistic, text-only benchmark that measures LLMs' ability to complete an assortment of tasks from distinct domains in a single prompt. The leading model, GPT-5.5 (xHigh), scores 43.3%. The test set entirely...

item.title: [AINews] AI Cybersecurity becomes top of mind
item.description: Several new Cyber headlines make us observe a trend

item.title: LG to Ban Residential Proxies from Smart TV Apps
item.description: The home appliance giant LG Electronics USA said this week it plans to suspend any apps built for its smart TVs that turn one's television into an always-on residential proxy node. The move comes less than a month after researchers found that more than 42 percent of games and other apps available...

item.title: Elevated errors for API image generation
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied mitigations and are seeing error rates return to normal levels. We are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Images (Operational) Image Generation (Operational)

item.title: Newly entitled R2 accounts are seeing delays when trying to access the S3-compatible API
item.description: Jul 21, 20:20 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 21, 17:31 UTC Identified - We have identified an issue with issuance of advanced certificates for new R2 users, customers may see a delay while we process a backlog.

item.title: The State of Simulation for Physical AI: An Overview
🔬Causal Models Need Causal Data - Xaira’s X-Cell model for Drug Discovery (Bo Wang & Ci Chu, Chief Discovery Officer & Chief AI Scientist)
item.description: Xaira Therapeutics is all in on data generation for model building! We talk with Bo Wang and Ci Chu about how and why.

item.title: Issues with logins and signups
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll affected API Platform functionality has now fully recovered.Affected components Login (Operational) Login (Operational)
